reviews.review_description: Glitches a lot and usually won't work
Great little title on the Go. I ran through a whole battery playing around with it. Get it, you won't regret it.

reviews.review_title: its not that bad, party game
reviews.review_description: sure the quality is homebrew at best, however the game mechanics are universal (its wii bowling). as long as there are no other bowling games on oculus (missed opportunity) ill give this a high rating.

reviews.review_title: Creative mashup of bowling and minigolf
reviews.review_description: This is by far my favorite game on the Go. Lots of fun that is challenging and well designed, with realistic physics in fantasy environments. I play it more frequently than any other game on my Go.

Honestly I don’t understand the negative reviews at all. This game is a really great bowling game with an awesome twist and tons of content. Some of the lanes are hit or miss but overall the variety is really well designed and impressive. To say this is the best bowling game on the Go is obvious. Shooter’s Alley is a silly diversion with nothing to it, but this game has loads of great content and replayability. There is a bit of a lack of polish and some bugginess but it’s definitely worth the pennies you pay for it.
this game is legit so fun. i love the modes and maps and the game feels nice and cozy. if you have 5 dollars to spend then this game is a MUST HAVE!
I can't believe I spent money on this!

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you didn't enjoy the game.  And feedback on what you did/didn't like would be greatly appreciated so I can make improvements in the future.  Thanks!

reviews.review_description: It was very hard to get used to this game but once you are it is a super fun breeze. The physics are great and I love the gravity changes.
I like the app the only thing is it's very difficult I haven't got a good score yet and I've been at it for like 2 days

I’ve only played this game twice, but it was a lot of fun.  It did get frustrating on a few holes where I couldn’t throw the ball so it actually went to the pins.  I’m guessing with some practice, it’ll be much easier.
I can see why you didn’t offer a free trial. This game is not very good. I’ll consider this an inexpensive lesson on which developers to avoid in the future. Enjoy the $5.
